title: Postseason preview: Matchups, biggest questions and what to know
content: Everything you need to know for the Thunder, Pistons, Spurs, Celtics and 16 other teams chasing the 2026 NBA title.

title: Offseason guides: Draft, free agency, trade targets for eliminated teams

title: Kerr on future: These jobs have 'expiration date'
content: Steve Kerr acknowledged his future with the Warriors was uncertain following a season-ending loss to the Suns, saying, "When the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas."

title: OKC remains title betting fave; Spurs, C's follow
content: The Thunder (+110) remain large betting favorites to repeat as NBA champions, but the betting public also likes the Spurs (+500) and Celtics (+550) to give Oklahoma City a run for its money.

title: Source: Texans, Anderson agree to $150M deal
content: Texans and All-Pro defensive end Will Anderson Jr. have agreed to a historic three-year, $150 million extension to make him the highest-paid non-quarterback, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Fever's Boston lands record $6.3M extension
content: Three-time WNBA All-Star Aliyah Boston has agreed to a four-year, $6.3 million contract extension with the Fever, her agent told ESPN.

title: Suns end Warriors' season, await No. 1 Thunder
content: The Suns knocked out the Warriors 111-96 to secure the No. 8 seed and will face the top-seeded Thunder in the first round of the playoffs.

title: Magic, driven by 'urgency,' rip Hornets to advance
content: The Magic built a 35-point lead in the first half of their 121-90 rout of the Hornets and advanced to a first-round playoff matchup against the Pistons.

title: Wesley sparks USWNT to 3-0 victory over Japan
content: Kennedy Wesley recorded her first international goal to lead the United States to a 3-0 friendly win over Japan on Friday night in Commerce City, Colorado.

title: Retired stars Bird, Rapinoe announce separation
content: Retired sports stars Sue Bird and Megan Rapinoe announced on social media that, after a decade together, they are no longer in a relationship.

title: 🏈 NFL mini mock draft: Predicting top 10 picks
content: We asked NFL scouts to make draft picks for other teams, assigning one to each top-10 slot. Which prospects could go early?

title: WrestleMania 42 roundtable: Most anticipated matches, bold predictions
content: Before we get to a weekend full of wrestling (Saturday and Sunday on ESPN Unlimited), our crew shares its thoughts on the must-see matches and more.

title: Inside the fallout of the Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel photos
content: How Dianna Russini and New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel scrambled to respond to photos published in the New York Post.

title: Riddick picks his favorite NFL draft prospects: The guys he'd want on his team from Rounds 1-3
content: Louis Riddick picks his guys in the 2026 class, including some offensive playmakers and ball-hawking defensive backs. See who's on his 10-player list.

title: One year later: Who won the Jaguars-Browns draft-day trade for Travis Hunter?
content: The Browns and Jags swapped picks in 2025 so Jacksonville could draft Hunter. Now, Cleveland has an extra first-rounder in 2026.

title: Revisiting each playoff matchup between LeBron James and Kevin Durant
content: James and Durant have faced off in the postseason three times, and each matchup proved to be a momentous occasion.

title: How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
content: The Lakers lost two of their three stars. Now they face Kevin Durant and the Houston Rockets -- as a home underdog.

title: The biggest question facing every WNBA team in training camp
content: How will Angel Reese fit the Dream? Will the Wings return to the playoffs? Can Caitlin Clark remain healthy?

title: What did the USWNT learn ahead of World Cup after Japan series?
content: The USWNT just wrapped its window of three friendly games against Japan, but what did Emma Hayes learn ahead of the World Cup?

content: We've got potential title deciders in England and Germany, a grudge match in Portugal, and the Copa del Rey final: Julien Laurens previews the weekend and makes his predictions.

title: Man City vs. Arsenal: What to watch for in Premier League title showdown
content: Arsenal's visit to Man City on Sunday will likely decide who wins the league. Here are the keys to the match.

title: Forget the 40: The true measure of Carnell Tate
content: Wide receiver Carnell Tate is confident, intelligent and ready to star in the NFL. His mother made sure of it.

title: Spring football games: Previewing Virginia Tech, Michigan, Miami and others
content: Spring football games are in full swing this weekend. Here's what to know ahead of Friday and Saturday.

content: The top of the 2026 MLB draft is coming into focus. Here's who your team could be targeting this summer.

content: It's playoff time in the NBA and NHL, and some teams are breaking out some wild concessions for fans to enjoy in the postseason.

title: Biggest haters funeral: Chris Paul pokes fun at Clippers after play-in tournament loss
content: The LA Clippers saw their season end Wednesday, and an ex-Clipper made sure to rub salt in the wound.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, strategies and more
content: Everything you need to know before your fantasy women's basketball draft, including rankings, tips and news.

title: 10 NFL draft prospects to put on your fantasy radar -- and three others to monitor
content: With the NFL draft nearly upon us, it's time to think about how the top prospects will fare in fantasy.

title: Mason Miller tops all pitchers? CJ Abrams goes 30-100-30? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool eye Wharton amid Man United, Real Madrid interest
content: Liverpool face competition from Manchester United and Real Madrid to sign Crystal Palace midfielder Adam Wharton. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: civic issues take centre stage as electors in Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni constituency seek permanent solutions
content: The DMK stronghold is set for a 4-cornered contest, with the ruling party and its allies, the AIADMK and BJP, TVK, and NTK in the fray. A few party representatives say they expect a narrow victory margin as it is likely that voters aged 18-30 may back parties such as TVK over the Dravidian majors

title: PM Modi says 'will get more chances' to push for women's quota, blames Congress as 'anti-reform party' | LIVE
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, in his address to the nation, said parties that opposed the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam in Parliament were taking women's power for granted.

title: Interlinking of rivers remains key poll issue among voters in Polur
content: More than 90 per cent of residents in the constituency depend on agriculture; however, Cheyyar river, which is a lifeline for residents in the region, remains seasonal

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Salem South constituency people want traffic congestion, mosquito menace, drinking water issues addressed
content: Salem South has a large number of weavers and people involved in the textile businesses in Ammapet, Dadagapatti, and Gugai. Silver anklet workers also constitute a significant number in Nethimedu, Maniyanoor, and Dadagapatti.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Untreated waste, poor civic infra mar Erode (East) Assembly constituency
content: Pollution, traffic congestion, and poor urban infrastructure remain key concerns for voters, who are looking for concrete outcomes

title: West Bengal Pradesh Congress says no post-poll alliance
content: Bengal Congress Committee chief tries to dispel reports on the possibility of post-poll tie-up with TMC; the Congress is contesting all Assembly seats on its own strength in the State, ending a decade-long understanding with the CPI(M)-led Left Front

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Coastal residents of Kanniyakumari and Nagercoil constituencies voice discontent
content: Around 15 coastal villages fall under the jurisdiction of the Kanniyakumari and Nagercoil constituencies but residents of most of them say they are dissatisfied with the projects implemented

title: T.N. Assembly election: Villivakkam, a densely-populated urban constituency, faces civic gaps despite infrastructure push
content: Home to a diverse electorate, comprising working-class sections, government employees, and small business owners, Villivakkam reflects the city’s evolving socio-economic landscape. A 24-hour govt. hospital and the replacement of the ageing sewer network are some of the demands of voters

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Corporation status fuels Karaikudi constituency’s demand for new district
content: As of the 2026 electoral rolls, Karaikudi remains the largest constituency in the district with approximately 3,21,068 voters, followed by Sivaganga and Tirupathur

title: CPI(M) activist Mostari Banu says it was her Supreme Court petition that secured relief for excluded voters in Bengal
content: Banu hails from humble origins in the Bhagawangola constituency of Murshidabad district, one of the worst affected by SIR deletions; CM Mamata Banerjee had earlier claimed her petition had resulted in the apex court’s order

title: Dharmavaram silk shines at expo in China
content: National award-winning designer Jujare Nagaraju showcases signature weaves at ‘Vasanthamela-2026’ exhibitions in Beijing and Guangzhou, China

title: Chief Minister N. Chandra Babu Naidu launches a national-level campaign against Opposition for defeating Constitution Amendment Bill
content: The Opposition parties defeated the bill citing adverse impact on small States, South and North Eastern States despite promise by Narendra Modi for the equal increase in the number of Lok Sabha seats: April 17 was a black day in Indian political history, said Mr. Naidu

title: UDF’s ‘surveillance squad’ keeps round-the-clock watch on EVM strongroom
content: Squad tasked with guarding the people’s mandate until counting day by monitoring CCTV footage from the strongroom at CUSAT, where electronic voting machines from the constituency are stored

title: 10 villages will part with 17,000 acres for Amaravati project, says A.P. CM
content: Terming Amaravati a land monetisation project, Naidu says that financial liabilities will be cleared by selling the stretches acquired under Land Pooling Scheme

title: Rural summer camp ‘Akkareya Angala’ launched for children in Mandya district of Karnataka
content: Mandya zilla panchayat Chief Executive Officer K. R. Nandini said the primary objective of the camp is to encourage reading, writing, creative arts, and special games. The initiative aims to ensure that children from rural areas utilise their summer holidays in an enjoyable and productive manner

title: ‘Why delay the vandan?’: Women’s reservation comes full circle as government’s ‘U-turn’ fails in Lok Sabha
content: With 131st Constitution Amendment Bill failing, implementation now remains contingent on Census and delimitation under 2023 Act

title: Neyveli braces for a battle of arch rivals
content: Though Neyveli has transformed from an agrarian belt into an industrial township, the contest in Neyveli is essentially framed around labourers, including regularisation of contract workmen appointed through the INDCOSERVE society with better wages

title: Crimes against women, children on the rise in A.P., alleges Jagan
content: YSRCP president accuses the State government of misusing institutions for political revenge, weakening law and order, and violating constitutional values

title: Southern Railway introduced 28 pairs of new trains in last fiscal
content: KSR Bengaluru–Ernakulam Vande Bharat Express and 10 pairs of Amrit Bharat Express trains among newly-introduced trains

title: Malappuram’s Pang village bids tearful farewell to nine victims of Valparai tragedy
content: Bodies were brought to Government Higher Secondary School, Pang, for public viewing after postmortem held at Pollachi; accident nearly orphans GLP School, Pang, leaving just one primary teacher, who could not join the excursion

title: Chhattisgarh power plant explosion death toll touches 23
content: Two more workers die during treatment; Lok Sabha MP Naveen Jindal comee out in support of Vedanta chairman

title: Calicut University launches placement and training centre
content: It is part of a project to utilise private contributions to improve academic facilities. Federal Bank contributes ₹18 lakh

title: A.P. IT Minister Nara Lokesh steps in to save infant with a rare disorder
content: He bridges the ₹6 crore funding gap, and facilitates a one-time gene therapy injection that costs nearly ₹16 crore

title: New BJP-led govt. in Bihar to undergo floor test on April 24
content: As the number of NDA legislators in the State Assembly stands at a whopping 201 and the Opposition 41 MLAs in the 243-member Assembly, the new government led by Samrat Choudhary is likely to sail through the floor test

title: No structural cracks in Wayanad township houses, says Revenue Minister K. Rajan
content: Minister inspects affected units in the township built for the 2024 landslide survivors and says the marks reported have been caused by water seepage

title: Entries invited for Abu Dhabi Shakti awards
content: Original literary works published between January 1, 2023, and December 31, 2025, will be considered for the award

title: Congress and its allies ‘celebrated’ setback to women’s 33% reservation push: BJP
content: BJP leaders Ravi Shankar Prasad and Smriti Irani accuse the Congress of a ‘history’ of neglecting women’s welfare and sabotaging its own Bills, and treating women as political tools rather than respecting them as equal citizens

title: Congress exposed ‘ulterior motive’ behind women’s reservation Bill, says CWC leader Gidugu Rudra Raju
content: NDA’s attempt to gain political mileage ahead of the elections in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal has been successfully thwarted by the Opposition, he says

title: War of words erupts in Karnataka over Women’s Reservation Bill
content: Siddaramaiah described the events that unfolded in Parliament as ‘deceptive political drama’ orchestrated by the NDA government; Karnataka BJP says it will hold protests against the Congress’s ‘anti-women’ stance

title: 'Dreams of nari shakti crushed despite our best effort': PM apologises to women - Top quotes
content: Prime Minister Modi expressed deep regret over the women's reservation bill's defeat, apologizing to the nation's women. He accused opposition parties like Congress, DMK, TMC, and SP of prioritizing self-interest over national progress and women's empowerment, highlighting their alleged celebration of the bill's failure as an insult to women's dignity.

title: OpenAI exec Srinivas Narayanan leaves to spend time with ageing parents in India
content: Srinivas Narayanan, OpenAI's CTO for B2B applications, is departing the company next week after nearly three years. He plans to visit his parents in India before considering future professional endeavors. Narayanan, who previously served as VP of Engineering, shared his departure on LinkedIn, calling his tenure at the AI firm 'three incredible years'.

title: Explained: On way to 4th largest, how India slipped to 6th rank & what it means
content: In fact, IMF’s latest World Economic Outlook (April 2026) sees India sitting at the sixth spot this financial year too. What has led to the sudden fall? Why has India dropped to the sixth position, falling behind the UK, instead of overtaking Japan to become the fourth largest economy?

title: INS Dhruv: India’s missile tracking ship, ensuring the country’s strategic might
content: India's INS Dhruv plays a crucial role in tracking long-range missile tests over the Bay of Bengal and Indian Ocean. This advanced vessel gathers vital telemetry data, aids in anti-ballistic missile system development, and provides early warning against hypersonic threats. Its capabilities also extend to monitoring Low Earth Orbit satellites, offering a significant strategic advantage to the nation's armed forces.

title: Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
content: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.

title: Young, overweight, at risk: India's silent health emergency
content: A new health report reveals over half of Indians under 30 are now overweight or obese, a concerning trend accelerating with sedentary lifestyles and poor diets. This early weight gain significantly increases the risk of metabolic diseases like fatty liver and diabetes at younger ages, posing a long-term public health challenge for the nation.

title: David Miller redeems himself with last-over finish as DC chase down 176 vs RCB
content: After losing three wickets in the powerplay, DC recovered through Rahul (57 off 34 balls), Stubbs (60 not out off 47 balls) and Axar Patel (26 retired hurt). Rahul steadied the innings after an early collapse and targeted Josh Hazlewood, hitting him for two sixes and three fours. He reached his fifty in 30 balls before falling to Krunal Pandya, caught by Virat Kohli at long-on.

title: China plans export ban to US after report warns how Tesla may prove to be nightmare
content: China is considering restricting exports of advanced solar panel manufacturing equipment to the US, targeting high-efficiency technologies like HJT. This move could significantly impact US efforts to localize solar production and space-based computing ambitions, including those of Tesla, amid escalating tech rivalry.

title: 'They made a big mistake': PM Modi directs ministers to expose opposition's 'anti-women' stand
content: Prime Minister Modi asserted that opposition parties will face repercussions for not backing the women's reservation bill, urging ministers to highlight their "anti-women" stance. He reiterated the government's commitment to implement the reservation by 2029, accusing the opposition of a "big mistake" and attempting to cover up their actions after the bill's defeat in Lok Sabha.

title: Allahabad HC stays FIR order against Rahul Gandhi in dual citizenship case
content: The Allahabad high court has temporarily halted its order for an FIR against Rahul Gandhi concerning alleged dual citizenship. The court emphasized the need to hear the accused before any decision. This follows an earlier directive to register a case based on claims of concealed British citizenship during company incorporation. The probe's transfer to a central agency was also permitted.

title: ‘No one can call me anti-women’: Shashi Tharoor shares lighthearted exchange with Kiren Rijiju
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor shared a lighthearted exchange with Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju, where Rijiju conceded Tharoor couldn't be labelled anti-women. This follows the defeat of a bill proposing 33% women's reservation in legislatures, which aimed to increase Lok Sabha seats. The government's proposal faced opposition, with the Congress criticising the timing and linkage to delimitation.

title: Tim David denies single, falls next ball as costly blunder triggers RCB slide
content: Tim David's refusal of a single proved costly as Royal Challengers Bengaluru faltered in their chase against Delhi Capitals. David's dismissal for 26 off 17 balls, after turning down a run, marked a significant shift in momentum. Earlier, a disciplined bowling effort from Delhi restricted RCB to 175 for 8, despite a strong start from Phil Salt.

title: ‘Troubles DMK, Congress to see ordinary women rise’: PM Modi at Coimbatore rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed pain and anger over the failure of the women's reservation bill, accusing the DMK, Congress, and their allies of engaging in "hatred and petty politics." He stated that the DMK "harms women" and "doesn't stand with women in parliament," predicting a response for their "anti-women stand"

title: Gunfire in Hormuz: Indian-flagged tanker among ships targeted as Iran closes strait again
content: Mixed signals from Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz have caused Indian and Greek crude tankers to turn back amid heightened tensions. Uncertainty over passage through the vital chokepoint deepened after conflicting reports from Tehran, with some merchant ships also reporting gunfire in the troubled waterway.

title: $50,000M revenue blow: What led to half a billion barrels being erased during Iran war
content: A nearly 50-day Iran war has crippled global oil markets, removing over 500 million barrels and costing $50 billion. This unprecedented energy shock, impacting Gulf producers severely, could have long-lasting effects. While the Strait of Hormuz has reopened and a ceasefire is anticipated, a full recovery for energy supplies is expected to be a slow, multi-year process.

title: Women quota bill fails in Lok Sabha: Strategic move or poor planning?
content: For the first time since 2014, the Modi government failed to pass a bill in Parliament, a move analysts suggest was strategic. By forcing a vote on the women's reservation bill, linked to delimitation, the BJP aimed to record every MP's stance, creating a potent campaign tool. The government positioned itself as pro-women reformists, while blaming opposition parties for obstructing progress. This tactic seeks to shape the political narrative ahead of crucial elections, highlighting intent versus obstruction.

title: ‘Samson will captain CSK’ - Ashwin makes bold claim on leadership shift
content: Ravichandran Ashwin sees Sanju Samson as a future captain for Chennai Super Kings. Ashwin believes Samson should forge his own path, not follow past leaders. The transition in CSK leadership has been ongoing. Samson has shown strong form recently. He will be a key player in upcoming matches.

title: Ahmed named head coach; Pakistan include 4 uncapped for B'desh Tests
content: Pakistan have named a 16-man squad for the Bangladesh Tests, led by Shan Masood, with Sarfaraz Ahmed as head coach. Sitting fifth in the WTC, they aim to improve against eighth-placed Bangladesh. Four uncapped players are included, alongside senior stars. A Karachi camp precedes the May 8 start, with matches in Dhaka and Sylhet.

title: Elderly Pakistani-origin couple found dead in Jaisalmer under suspicious circumstances
content: An elderly Pakistani-origin displaced couple was found dead under suspicious circumstances in their Jaisalmer home, with preliminary findings suggesting strangulation. The disappearance of the woman's silver anklets points towards a possible robbery motive. The couple, who had recently obtained Indian citizenship, lived alone and had no children.

title: DA hike: Govt approves 2% dearness allowance raise for central govt employees
content: Central government employees will see a 2% Dearness Allowance increase, approved by the Cabinet. This comes as employee bodies push for significant pay structure reforms under the proposed 8th Pay Commission, seeking a higher fitment factor and expanded family definitions for pay calculations.

title: Chasing likes, losing lives: How social media reels are fueling fatal risks among youth
content: Tragic incidents across India highlight the deadly risks of filming social media reels. Teenagers and young adults are losing their lives in crashes, drownings, and fatal falls while chasing viral fame. Experts and authorities urge greater awareness, parental supervision, and platform responsibility to curb this dangerous trend before more lives are lost.

title: PSL turns comedy show: Replacement player plays alongside player he replaced
content: A bizarre selection mix-up in the Pakistan Super League saw a replacement player feature alongside the one he was meant to replace. Quetta Gladiators mistakenly included both Dinesh Chandimal and Bevon Jacobs in their Playing XI due to a late illness to Tom Curran, causing social media outrage.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection: Can Ranveer Singh film dethrone 'Pushpa 2's domestic collection?
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' also known as 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has cemented its status as a cultural phenomenon, effectively redefining the "spy universe" in Indian cinema. As of April 18, 2026, the film is completing its first month in theaters, having shattered nearly every domestic record held by previous blockbusters. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the 229-minute espionage masterpiece is now setting its sights on an even more audacious target: the all-time Indian domestic box office record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore net currently held by Allu Arjun’s 'Pushpa 2: The Rule.'

title: 'Shouldn't be considered failure of govt ...': Rijiju on women's reservation bill not clearing LS
content: The government faced a setback as the women's reservation bill failed to pass in the Lok Sabha, requiring a two-thirds majority. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju blamed the Congress and opposition parties, calling it an anti-women stance and a significant blow to the nation. The opposition, while supporting reservation, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

title: 'Black day for Centre, they felt a shock for first time': Priyanka on delimitation bill defeat
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra hailed the defeat of the Delimitation Bill as a victory for democracy, accusing the Centre of a conspiracy to alter the federal structure. The bill, which proposed women's reservation linked to delimitation, failed to secure the required majority in the Lok Sabha. Opposition parties, while supporting women's reservation in principle, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

title: Not CSK or MI, RCB to become first ever IPL team to achieve rare milestone
content: The match at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium will be an afternoon game on the day the tournament completes 19 years.

title: PSL drama! Afridi loses temper, smashes bat in outburst - Watch
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Shah Afridi displayed visible anger after being dismissed for a duck against Quetta Gladiators in the PSL 2026. His frustration, shown by slamming his bat, highlights the defending champions' inconsistent campaign and mounting pressure as they fight for playoff contention.

title: TCS Nashik case: No complaints through POSH channels, absconding female employee; what we know
content: A fact-finding committee has launched an inquiry into alleged sexual harassment and religious conversion at a Nashik TCS office. The panel, including a retired judge and IPS officer, will interview all stakeholders. Meanwhile, Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis called it a "very serious matter," investigating potential "corporate jihad." Police are searching for an accused employee.

title: 'Congress And Its Allies Have Been Unmasked': PM's Top Quotes On Women's Bill
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the nation on Saturday, a day after the Women's Reservation Bill was defeated in Parliament. Earlier in the day, PM Modi told his Cabinet that the Opposition had committed a mistake by blocking the bill.

title: DMK Citadel Chepauk In Focus As Udhayanidhi Stalin Seeks Re-Election
content: In 2021, Udhayanidhi Stalin made his electoral debut from Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni, winning by a margin of more than 60,000 votes.

title: Noida Violence Unraveled: 3 Key Conspirators Identified, 2 Arrested
content: TheNoidaPolice have claimed a major breakthrough in the recentviolenceinvolving labourers in Gautam Buddh Nagar, stating that the unrest was the result of a well-orchestrated conspiracy

title: 2 Including Retired Soldier Killed In Highway Ambush In Manipur's Ukhrul
content: The killings happened just a day after Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh visited the district and spoke about providing security for common people to use the highway safely

title: 'Debate With Trump Doesn't Interest Me At All': Pope Leo
content: An example was a speech about "tyrants" ransacking the world that he delivered in Cameroon on the second leg of a tour of Africa, said Pope Leo.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Conveyed "Deep Concern" To Iran Envoy: India On Firing On 2 Ships In Hormuz
content: US-Iran War Live: The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases.

title: Noble Effort Derailed Due To Opposition's Petty Politics: PM On Women's Bill
content: PM Modi also attacked DMK for its staunch opposition to the delimitation exercise, saying the number of Lok Sabha seats in Tamil Nadu would have increased, but the ruling party blocked the central government's move.

title: 16-Year-Old Tribal Girl Raped By 5 In Jharkhand: Police
content: A 16-year-old tribal girl was allegedly raped by five youths in Jharkhand's Latehar district, a police officer said on Saturday.

title: Economy Boost, Health Gains: How More Women In Parliament Could Help India
content: It is estimated that the world is still 169 years away from achieving full gender parity in politics.

title: "Fell Unconscious At Work": TCS Nashik Employee's Grandmother Recounts "Toxicity"
content: The impact of the ordeal has extended far beyond the workplace, threatening her personal life just months after her marriage, the family told NDTV

title: 2 Indian-Flagged Ships Fired Upon In Hormuz: What We Know, In 5 Points
content: Two Indian-flagged vessels came under attack from Iranian gunboats in the Strait of Hormuz today, sources said. No one was injured and the vessels were not damaged, they said. The two ships had to turn back after the incident northeast of Oman.

title: "Tweets A Lot, Talks A Lot": Iran Minister Slams Trump Over Hormuz Blockade
content: "The American side tweets a lot, talks a lot. Sometimes confusing, sometimes, you know, contradictory," Khatibzadeh said, referring to US President Donald Trump and his frequent social media posts.

title: Maharashtra 'Love Trap' Accused Says Adopted 'Playboy' Persona After Breakup
content: One police official and two constables have been suspended after videos showed them celebrating Ayaz's birthday.

title: Before Boiler Blast, Chattisgarh's Worst Industrial Disaster Took Place At Vedanta Plant
content: Since the formation of the Chhattisgarh state, numerous industrial accidents have occurred in succession, a majority of these at Vedanta facilities.

title: 2027 recruiting class rankings: Oklahoma among top 10 schools off to strong start
content: more than 60% of the players in the SC Next 300 remain uncommitted . the summer months will be pivotal in shaping the rankings . eight teams that finished with a top-10 class for the 2026 cycle have picked up where they left off with classes again near the top nationally .

title: Who's winning the Stanley Cup? All the intel you need on the 16 playoff teams
content: the Eastern Conference bracket was set earlier this week, including the Carolina Hurricanes and Buffalo Sabres as division champions . the Edmonton Oilers and Anaheim Ducks will face off as the Nos. 2 and 3 seeds, while the Los Angeles Kings will play the Avalanche as the second wild card .

title: How the Stanley Cup will be won: Wyshynski's predictions for the entire bracket
content: the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs are here, and they're weird . the Buffalo Sabres , who last made the playoffs when "Fast Five" was in theaters, are here . but that's why the tournament is the greatest postseason in sports: the unpredictability .

title: 19 going on 35: How Konnor Griffin's maturity is making an impression on Pirates
content: the 19-year-old Pittsburgh Pirates rookie gave Konnor Griffin a franchise-record contract extension . he struck out on five pitches, three of which were over 100 mph . "now, I've done it," an analyst says .
